lesson.menu_desc: Start, Build & Keep a Professional Conversation -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 5 -- Small Talk That Works -- Conversation Starters & Question Forms
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Simone Quiles de Santana Marques | Aula 5 -- Small Talk That Works

listenings.text: Hi Simone, this is Daniel Brooks. We met for a minute at the legal conference this morning, near the coffee table, but we did not really get to know each other. I wanted to break the ice properly, so I am leaving you this quick voice note. It turns out we have a lot in common: you work on cross-border deals, and so do I. During the panel, you brought up a great point about contract risk, and I would love to catch up and hear more. There is a networking lunch tomorrow at one o'clock. If you are free, it would be a perfect chance to talk. Either way, let us keep in touch. Here is my number. Talk soon, and enjoy the rest of the conference.

Hi, this is Simone. I am leaving a quick note for my team after two days at the legal conference. Honestly, networking felt hard at first, but a few simple things really helped. First, I learned to break the ice with an easy question instead of talking about work right away. A good conversation starter, like asking what brought someone to the event, opens the door fast. Second, I tried to find something in common before bringing up business, and people relaxed. Third, when a chat was going well, I asked to catch up later and we agreed to keep in touch. I met three lawyers who work on similar deals. I will share their contacts this afternoon, so we can wrap up our networking list by Friday. Have a great day.

extra_audio.text: Simone arrives at the legal conference and feels nervous about networking. Daniel breaks the ice with a friendly question at the coffee break. They find they have a lot in common and talk about their work. Simone brings up a point from the panel, and they agree to catch up at lunch. Before they leave, they exchange numbers and promise to keep in touch.
